evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: ensure data quality, consistency, and documentation; data governance and best practice improvements
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Classified as rigour because responsibilities centre on 'ensure data quality, consistency, and documentation', 'data governance', and 'best practice improvements' — with no velocity or speed-to-ship signals.
evidence.domain_risk: IT consultancy (Software Development and IT Services); no regulated sector named
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Classified as moderate because Size Up Consulting is an IT services consultancy without named regulated industry exposure, where errors affect client business decisions without direct regulatory consequence.
evidence.data_team_maturity: 200 IT experts; 5-year average employee tenure; named sub-teams (Data Engineering, Data Analytics)
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Classified as mature because the firm has 200 IT experts, an established team structure with named sub-teams (Data Engineering, Data Analytics), and a 5-year average tenure indicating stability.
evidence.jd_authorship: design and maintain data models for analytical purposes; ensure data quality, consistency, and documentation; support teams in data and decision-making tool utilisation
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Classified as recruiter because responsibilities are generic analytics engineering boilerplate without named tools in responsibilities, specific scale figures, or context — they could apply to any AE role at any consultancy.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: central bridge between Data Engineering, Data Analytics, and end users; semantic models for activity management
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Classified as internal_data because the primary role framing is serving internal data consumers — 'central bridge between Data Engineering, Data Analytics, and end users'.
evidence.autonomy_level: design and maintain data models; participate in data governance and best practice improvements; support teams
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Classified as mixed because 'design data models' and 'participate in governance improvements' signal some strategic input while 'support teams in data and decision-making tool utilisation' signals execution.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool usage or AI infrastructure enablement is mentioned anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: ensure data quality, consistency, and documentation
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Classified as responsibility because 'ensure data quality, consistency, and documentation' is an ownership verb framing data quality as a named candidate accountability.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: reliable, consistent, and accessible data; data governance and best practice improvements
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Classified as moderate because 'reliable, consistent, and accessible data' is the role's stated mission but the framing is delivery-oriented without compliance, audit, or trust-failure language.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: design and maintain data models; optimise data model and analytical query performance
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Classified as fix_scale because the role is primarily about maintaining and optimising existing data models and transformations, not building a new platform from scratch.
evidence.loss_aversion: reliable, consistent, and accessible data — data reliability is the stated mission but no compliance or audit framing present.
